---
title: "Duino concept map (prototype)"
draft: true
---

{{< include /_includes/_post_head.qmd >}}

{{< include _glossary.qmd >}}

{{< include /_includes/_post_mid.qmd >}}

# Duino concept map (prototype)

[A first pass at the orientation map. Three things to react to: (1) the layout / what is foregrounded, (2) whether the labels render as clickable links in your preview, (3) where this should live — top of the core page, the series homepage, or a hover/tooltip on every page.]{.post-subtitle}

{{< include _concept-map.qmd >}}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Notes for iteration:

- Regime axis (R1/R2/R3) and Materialization mode axis (A/B/C) are **independent** — a discrete ensemble can be stored as enumeration or deltas; a continuous landscape is usually Mode C. The map shows them as parallel columns rather than a grid to keep it legible; we can show the cross-product later if useful.
- Links currently point at the present chapter files (`02-architecture.qmd#...`). If we collapse the core into a single document, these become `#sec-...` anchors and stop being cross-file.
- Whether the labels are actually clickable in the rendered page depends on the SVG backend the `diagram` filter uses (dvisvgm keeps `\href`; pdf2svg drops it). If they don't survive, the fallback is to render this one figure as inline SVG/HTML where links are guaranteed.
:::

{{< include /_includes/_layout_end.qmd >}}
